title: pepkio-dose-curve-fitter v0.1.0 – a Python client for dose-response curve fitting via the Pepkio life-science calculator suite
description: A frozen release of **pepkio-dose-curve-fitter** documents a citable open-source lab calculator API wrapper for four-parameter and five-parameter logistic dose–response models, IC50 and pIC50 estimation, and per-compound QC grading in pharmacology and high-throughput screening workflows. This Zenodo record preserves v0.1.0 so investigators can reproduce the exact client–API interactions used when fitting concentration–response data at scale. Source code and the issue tracker are maintained at https://github.com/pepkio/pepkio-dose-curve-fitter. The primary interactive platform for the same calculations is the [Pepkio Dose-Response Curve Fitter](https://www.pepkio.com/tools/dose-curve-fitter), part of a Pepkio suite of more than thirty web tools covering solution prep, PCR, cell culture, assays, pharmacology, education, and sequence utilities. The Python client supports batch compound processing and integration with Jupyter, Nextflow, and Snakemake without embedding calculator logic locally. Released under the MIT License; Pepkio (https://www.pepkio.com/) develops laboratory calculators and analysis services for life science research.
notes:
**Suggested citation.** When referencing the archived client in a publication, cite the Zenodo DOI together with the GitHub repository (https://github.com/pepkio/pepkio-dose-curve-fitter) and record the client version (v0.1.0) in methods or supplementary materials.

**Programmatic access.** This client is the recommended interface for scripted calls to the Pepkio Dose-Response Curve Fitter API; the interactive web application remains available for manual curve fitting. Calculator logic resides on Pepkio servers; this package transmits parameters and returns structured results only.
